ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311737 covers milk tea as a general category; no specific lychee-flavoured milk tea term exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKV covers tea-based beverages broadly; no specific milk tea or lychee milk tea node exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 represents milk tea as a substance class; lychee-flavoured variant is not individually coded. Closest parent used.

preparation_method: flavoured with lychee syrup or lychee puree
served chilled or at room temperature

serving.description: 1 medium cup (approx. 500 ml / 500 g as served with ice)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard medium serving of lychee milk tea at KOI Thé is approximately 500 ml, typically consumed as a single beverage portion.

nutrient_source.title: Milk tea, ready-to-drink, with sugar — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods; supplemented with lychee syrup composition from USDA Branded Food entries and HPB Singapore nutrient data for sweetened milk tea beverages
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for lychee milk tea exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by combining: (1) HPB Singapore data for sweetened milk tea (approximately 45–55 kcal/100 ml), (2) USDA FoodData Central data for brewed black tea with milk and sugar, and (3) lychee syrup contribution from USDA Branded Food entries. The medium KOI Thé serving is approximately 500 ml. Per-100 g values are derived from this composite estimate. Confidence is medium due to absence of an exact branded entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened milk tea and analogous sugar-sweetened beverages; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened tea beverages.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for lychee milk tea. GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened milk tea and sugar-sweetened tea beverages, which report GI values in the range 50–60. A GI of 55 is classified as 'low'. Lychee syrup contributes additional simple sugars consistent with this range. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 49 g) / 100 ≈ 26.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~90 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Lychee milk tea contains approximately 90 mg sodium per medium serving, which is low; however, regular consumption of sweetened beverages may contribute to weight gain and indirectly elevate blood pressure, so moderation is advised for individuals with hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (~46 g per 500 ml serving) and low glycemic index (~55) with high glycemic load (~27)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lychee milk tea delivers approximately 46 g of free sugars per medium serving with a low GI of 55, producing a high glycemic load of approximately 27; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and consider sugar-free or reduced-sugar variants.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar and saturated fat from dairy creamer
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of lychee milk tea contributes significant added sugars and small amounts of saturated fat from milk or non-dairy creamer, which may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk when consumed frequently; limiting intake to occasional consumption is recommended for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lychee Milk Tea (Medium) is a sweetened Taiwanese-style milk tea beverage flavoured with lychee syrup or lychee puree, served chilled or at room temperature. It is prepared by brewing black tea, blending it with milk or non-dairy creamer, adding lychee flavouring and sugar, and serving over ice in a medium cup of approximately 500 ml. The drink has a floral, sweet, and mildly fruity taste with a creamy, smooth texture characteristic of milk tea. In Singapore, it is widely available at Taiwanese tea chains such as KOI Thé and is a popular afternoon or casual beverage. Nutritionally, a medium serving provides approximately 240 kcal, 49 g of carbohydrates (predominantly free sugars), 4 g of fat, and 3 g of protein. The high sugar content gives it a low glycemic index of approximately 55 and a high glycemic load of approximately 27 per serving, making it a significant concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. The sodium content is low (~90 mg per serving), but the saturated fat from dairy creamer warrants moderation for cardiovascular health. The beverage is vegetarian and halal-certified at most outlets. Chinese name: 荔枝奶茶 (lì zhī nǎi chá). Malay: Teh Susu Leci. Tamil: லிச்சி பால் தேநீர். Hindi: लीची दूध चाय.

confidence_reason: No exact branded entry for KOI Thé Lychee Milk Tea exists in HPB Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are estimated from composite sources: HPB Singapore sweetened milk tea data, USDA FoodData Central brewed tea with milk and sugar entries, and USDA Branded Food lychee syrup data. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database for analogous sweetened milk tea beverages. Confidence is medium rather than low because the composite estimation methodology is well-supported and the food category is nutritionally well-characterised.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of beverage as served (liquid weight including ice melt contribution). Medium serving size estimated at 500 g/ml based on standard KOI Thé medium cup volume. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by product formulation for non-dairy creamer and tea base; no ruminant fat present in significant quantity. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no intact plant fibre; lychee syrup contributes negligible fibre. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data for this beverage category. 5. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 6. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 7. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 9. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 10. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 11. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 12. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 13. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 14. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the composite source data. 15. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened milk tea; it is classified as 'low'. No direct measurement for lychee milk tea was found. 16. Halal status reflects KOI Thé Singapore outlet practice; individual outlet certification should be verified. 17. Sugar content may vary significantly depending on sweetness level selected (0%, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%); values represent standard 100% sweetness as default. 18. The food_name 'Lychee Milk Tea (Medium)' is used as the canonical English name; 'M-Lychee Milk Tea' from the input is the menu-style designation preserved in aliases.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built complete record from scratch. Assigned canonical English food_name. Added all four mandatory multilingual names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) for Taiwanese/Singapore-served cuisine. Estimated nutrients from USDA FoodData Central and HPB Singapore composite. Set GI=55 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency. Set ontology match_type values to only permitted values (exact/closest_parent/none). Confirmed zero justifications for dietary_fibre_g and trans_fat_g in notes. Result: FAIL due to incorrect GI category for GI 55.

Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per standard GI classification (GI <= 55 is low). Updated related descriptions in gi_source.source_match_notes, health_context.type2_diabetes.key_factor and guidance, llm_training.natural_language_description, and notes. Updated veritas_qc to reflect the fix and new score.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
